rulings.Case Title: JOHN POPPIN VS. GEORGE V CRESSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 29, 2012, line 8, DEFENDANT PETER RADIN's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Plaintiff waived his objection based on insufficient notice by contesting the motion on the merits. The September 6, 2011 proof of service and the attached declaration of diligence state that Mr. Coffey was authorized to accept service, yet Mr. Coffey denies that and there is no other evidence to the contrary. Moreover, substituted service is only effective if the papers that needed to be served were mailed to Mr. Radin and Mr. Radin denies receiving them and the September 6, 2011 proof of service states that a declaration of mailing is attached, but the copy of the proof of service submitted on this motion has no declaration of mailing attached. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALLA ESAULTSEVA VS. GREGORY ROVNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearingto Vacate Order Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 29, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF ALLA ESAULTSEVA'S Notice Of Hearing to Vacate Order Of Plaintiff - Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed by Defendant; motion granted. The Court notes that Plaintiff's former counsel did submit an opposition, however, the Court finds that she has no standing to contest this motion. Plaintiff has made a decision to waive the sanctions award apparently to facilitate settlement, and it is not her former counsel's place to object to that decision. While Plaintiff may owe her former counsel outstanding fees, the appropriate venue for recovery is via a separate action (perhaps in Small Claims court since the amount at issue is approximately $1,000), not through an order for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302

rulings.Case Title: NANCY WRIGHT VS. MERCY MEDICAL CENTER MERCED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 29, 2012, line 13, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S EMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The second cause of action fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for elder abuse. Plaintiff failed to allege any reckless, oppressive, fraudulent, or malicious conduct by Defendant as required by Welfare & Institutions Code section 15657, and it does not appear that Plaintiff can cure the deficiency. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ENRIQUE PEDROZA et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 29, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFFS ENRIQUE PEDROZA AND LAILA PORTUGAL'S MOTION To Compel Further Disco Responses Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that it is appropriate to appoint a Discovery Referee for this case. The parties are directed to meet and confer and attempt to mutually agree upon an individual who can be appointed by the Court as the Discovery Referee. The motion to compel is ordered off calendar pending a hearing before the Discovery Referee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 29, 2012, line 22, dEFENDANTs THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES, GREG DEROSA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Moot as to the first cause of action for negligence, sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for FEHA and the fifth cause of action for violation of the Bane Act. The first cause of action is alleged only against Doe 1.As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiffs need to comply with CRC 2.112 and segregate the distinct claims of harassment, discrimination, and retaliation. Plaintiffs should also plead more facts regarding the protected activity they allegedly engaged in and the adverse employment actions they allegedly suffered. Plaintiffs cannot plead discrimination and retaliation claims against the individual defendants. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff needs to plead more facts to support the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
